rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when referring to component ports, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax for AGREE contract blocks.
generic_rules: Align helper declaration types with actual expression types (bool, int, real) to avoid type mismatches.
Avoid encoding AADL properties like timing or unit tokens directly in AGREE expressions.
Do not generate clauses for Boolean domain values unless the port is explicitly an AGREE bool.
Reference only visible identifiers from the AADL model in AGREE annexes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; implementation annexes for assignments.
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports directly in component type contracts without proper variable representation.
Do not attempt to translate AADL property names (e.g., Deadline, Period) into AGREE expressions unless typed ports exist.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and their automatic inclusion in AGREE scope
Model fusion and insertion location decisions
